_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-leg batch (Senate SD-15/16/17 + House HD-16/17): lavon-bracy-davis (SD-15 D, won Sept-2025 special for unexpired term ending 2026, progressive — pro-abortion-access + Equality FL slate + Voting-Rights-Act sponsor + gun-violence-prevention sponsor), darryl-ervin-rouson (SD-16 D, CURRENCY FIX: TERM-LIMITED, NOT seeking reelection, leaving office Nov 2026 for New College; HARD flsenate roll calls DISCONFIRM the 'more pro-life than caucus' hypothesis — NAY on both abortion bans/vouchers/permitless-carry/Don't-Say-Gay/anti-sanctuary), carlos-guillermo-smith (SD-17 D, CURRENCY FIX: NOT up until 2028; openly-LGBTQ progressive, Equality FL senior policy advisor, multi-year AWB prime sponsor, PP-PAC endorsed), kiyan-michael (HD-16 R, consistent conservative, 6 HARD roll calls all YEA: SB300/SB254/HB1/HB3/HB543/SB4-C + immigration sponsorships; Angel Mom), jessica-baker (HD-17 R, consistent conservative former prosecutor; HARD SB300+SB264 YEA, FOP-endorsed, prime sponsor pro-LEO HB17 + child-protection HB305; guns/vouchers/E-Verify left SOFT/null where individual roll calls weren't isolated).

set.office: State Senator (SD-15 · won Sept-2025 special election for unexpired term, on 2026 ballot)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: assumed SD-15 (Orlando/Orange) Sept 2, 2025 via special election to succeed the late Sen. Geraldine Thompson; previously held FL House HD-40 (2022-2025). Won the unexpired term (ends 2026), so on the 2026 ballot. Scored from documented posture evidence (abortion-travel-fund participation, Equality FL pro-choice slate, prime sponsorship of the FL Voting Rights Act, S702 gun-violence-prevention sponsorship). Per-bill House roll calls (HB1/HB543/abortion bans) were not individually retrievable this sweep and are left null rather than inferred from party.

lavon-bracy-davis@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Progressive Orlando Democrat. Sanctity: participated (via Greater Orlando NOW) in a 2022 Central FL Democratic initiative funding women's out-of-state abortion travel; on Equality Florida's 2026 pro-choice/pro-LGBTQ legislative slate (slate requires pro-choice). Marriage/sexuality: Equality FL slate sponsor and spoke against an anti-LGBTQ bill in committee (2026 Resistance Report) — supports LGBTQ in policy. Elections: prime SENATE sponsor of the Harry T. & Harriette V. Moore FL Voting Rights Act (HB1419/SB1598) providing same-day + automatic voter registration and expanded ballot access. Guns: sponsor S702 'Mental Health Programs for Gun Violence Prevention'. No cross-party deviation found. (NOTE: confirm office/sources point to her SD-15 Senate page, not the stale House page.)

0.note: Participated (via Greater Orlando NOW) in 2022 abortion-travel-funding initiative; on Equality FL pro-choice slate — supports abortion access

1.note: Pro-choice (Equality FL slate requires it); opposes abortion bans/abolition

4.note: No documented PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money; not inferred (finance pull recommended)

1.note: On Equality Florida pro-LGBTQ legislative slate — supports same-sex marriage/nondiscrimination

4.note: Spoke against an anti-LGBTQ bill (2026); Equality FL slate sponsor — supports LGBTQ promotion in policy

1.note: Prime sponsor FL Voting Rights Act (same-day + automatic voter registration) — contrary to photo-ID/citizenship-verification tightening

3.note: Sponsors expanded ballot-access / automatic registration — contrary to tightening mail-in/access

0.note: No clean affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar; no defund statement either

2.note: No individual anti-sanctuary roll call isolated; not inferred

0.note: No individual HB543 (2023) roll call isolated

1.note: Sponsor S702 'Mental Health Programs for Gun Violence Prevention' — supports gun-control regulation

set.office: State Senator (SD-16 · TERM-LIMITED, not seeking reelection — leaving office Nov 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-16 (St. Petersburg/Pinellas) senator since 2016 (House 2008-2016); hits FL's 8-year Senate limit, term ends Nov 2026. Announced he will NOT seek another office (declined a House HD-62 run) and is leaving the Legislature for a New College of Florida position — NOT a 2026 candidate. All high-value votes scored from official flsenate.gov roll-call PDFs (verified 'N Rouson'). DATA-INTEGRITY NOTE: freedomindex.us/legislator/3407 reports the OPPOSITE of his actual votes on HB5/SB300/HB543 — do NOT use it; the official PDFs govern.

darryl-ervin-rouson@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Faith/recovery personal background but a reliably Democratic voting record — the 'more pro-life than his caucus' hypothesis is DISCONFIRMED by primary roll calls. HARD flsenate.gov NAY votes: HB5 (2022 15-week ban), SB300 (2023 6-week ban), HB1557 (2022 Don't Say Gay), HB1 (2023 universal vouchers), SB1808 (2022 anti-sanctuary), HB543 (2023 permitless carry); opposed the 2025 'TRUMP Act' immigration bills on the floor. Equality Florida-endorsed. Only conservative-adjacent artifacts are weak/abandoned: a 2008 same-sex-adoption stance he publicly reversed, and a NAY on the 2018 post-Parkland SB7026 package (a gun-control bill — his NAY coincides with gun-rights only incidentally, NOT credited). CURRENCY FIX: term-limited, not on the 2026 ballot.

0.note: NAY HB5 (15-week ban) and NAY SB300 (6-week ban) — opposes abortion restrictions

4.note: No documented PAC money; not inferred

0.note: 2008 same-sex-adoption opposition reversed; current stance pro-LGBTQ — no clean affirmation

1.note: Equality Florida-endorsed; 'leading voice' for statewide LGBTQ nondiscrimination — does not oppose same-sex marriage

4.note: NAY HB1557 (2022) Don't Say Gay / Parental Rights in Education — opposes LGBTQ restrictions in schools

0.note: NAY HB1 (2023) universal vouchers — opposes school choice
Personal faith background but no policy vote located
Criminal-justice-REFORM record (addiction/re-entry), not back-the-blue; no clean cell met

2.note: NAY SB1808 (2022) anti-sanctuary/immigration-enforcement; opposed 2025 'TRUMP Act' on the floor

1.note: NAY SB7026 (2018) was a vote against a gun-CONTROL package bundled w/ school-marshal provisions; not credited as pro-gun

set.office: State Senator (SD-17 · seat next up 2028, not on 2026 ballot)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-17 (Orlando/Orange) senator since Nov 5, 2024 (qualified unopposed); 4-year term ends Nov 2028 — NOT on the 2026 ballot (CURRENCY FIX; record wrongly said seeking reelection 2026). Prior FL House 2016-2022 (lost HD-37 in 2022). First openly LGBTQ Latino FL legislator. Scored from HARD votes/sponsorships during his House tenure + current advocacy; some 2023 bills fell during his out-of-office gap and are not scored as votes.

carlos-guillermo-smith@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Leading progressive; no cross-party deviation. Sanctity: voted NO on HB5 (2022 15-week ban), authored pro-choice op-ed, Planned Parenthood PAC-endorsed 2024. Marriage/sexuality: led floor opposition to HB1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay'), opposed the transgender-sports ban, is/was Senior Policy Advisor at Equality Florida — uniformly pro-LGBTQ. Guns (signature issue): lead House sponsor of an assault-weapons + large-capacity-magazine ban every year 2016-2022 (his first-ever filed bill was an AWB); built his profile on Pulse-nightclub gun-violence response. Immigration: filed the 2025 'Florida Economic Prosperity and Immigration Act' (pro-immigrant), long sanctuary-city advocacy, called enforcement 'racism' (2025). Out of office for the 2023 HB1/SB300/HB543 votes — those left null, not inferred.

'Angel Mom' (son Brandon killed 2007 by a twice-deported illegal immigrant). Scored from SIX HARD official flsenate.gov House roll-call PDFs (verified 'Y Michael-16') plus prime sponsorships. No cross-party deviation found.

kiyan-michael@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Consistent Christian conservative. HARD flsenate.gov YEA roll calls: SB300 (2023 6-week abortion ban), SB254 (2023 minor gender-care ban), HB1 (2023 universal vouchers), HB3 (2024 porn age-verification), HB543 (2023 permitless carry), SB4-C (2025 immigration special-session/anti-sanctuary). Immigration is her signature issue: prime sponsor HB1307 (mandatory death penalty for unauthorized aliens convicted of capital crimes) and HB197 (employment eligibility/E-Verify); 2026 co-sponsor of an English-only driver's-license-test bill. NRA-endorsed (2022); DeSantis-endorsed + $50K from Friends of Ron DeSantis. Campaign: 'We support the Blue.' Christian (ministry degree). No anti-life/pro-gun-control/pro-LGBTQ vote, statement, or endorsement surfaced.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-17 (Duval/Mandarin/Southside) incumbent since 2022, re-elected 2024; Ballotpedia confirms running in the Nov 3, 2026 general. Former Seventh-Circuit prosecutor; husband Tim Baker is a GOP consultant. Scored from HARD individual roll calls where isolated (SB300, SB264) + confirmed prime sponsorships + FOP endorsement. HB543 (guns), HB1 (vouchers), SB1718 (E-Verify) were only confirmable as party-bloc passage, NOT her isolated vote — left null per discipline, not inferred from party. No cross-party deviation found.

jessica-baker@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Consistent conservative former prosecutor. HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week abortion ban); YEA SB264 (2023 foreign-land / CCP property ban). Public justice (strongest category): prime sponsor HB17 (2026 'Criminal Offenses Against Law Enforcement Officers'), Fraternal Order of Police-endorsed, prime sponsor HB305 (2024 child-protection / sex-trafficking — raises child-victim hearsay admissibility, mandates sexual-predator designation). Co-sponsored the 2024 ~$1B tax-cut package. NOT a sponsor of the 2023 gender-care ban (HB1421). Marriage/sexuality, Christian liberty, election-integrity, and 10th-Amendment cells are unevidenced and left null. Thin but honest evidence_state.
